setting.blog.vali.ok.metaweblog: The configuration has been saved and verified
setting.blog.vali.tip.metaweblog: The configuration is invalid or the API is unavailable. Refresh the page if you changed the config.
setting.blog.vali.error: API validation failed, please check the configuration
setting.blog.vali.failed.check: Validation failed, please check the platform configuration
setting.blog.middlewareUrl.tip: Required in some scenarios such as self-hosting, Nginx server mode, or Vite dev server mode. Ignore it in other cases.
setting.blog.middlewareUrl.my.tip: Available in some cross-origin scenarios. It cannot bypass browser-restricted headers such as origin or referer, and the request body does not support FormData or other binary formats. Self-hosting is recommended for better performance. You can deploy https://github.com/terwer/node-metaweblog-api-adaptor .
setting.blog.middlewareUrl.my.fee.tip: Because a shared proxy is expensive and may be discontinued unexpectedly, self-hosting is recommended for service continuity. You can also support the project with a coffee to help keep the shared proxy available.
setting.blog.corsAnywhereUrl.tip: A new global CORS proxy introduced in 1.20.0+. It has higher priority and will override the old proxy setting above once configured.
setting.blog.middlewareUrl.my.new.tip: The new CORS proxy solves both network-access and browser CORS issues. It supports global cross-origin requests and FormData. Docker deployments must configure a CORS proxy for most scenarios.
setting.blog.middlewareUrl.my.warn.tip: Special note: Request limits are 100,000/day and 1,000/10 minutes. Please avoid abusive traffic or the shared proxy may be shut down.
setting.blog.init.error: Failed to read the initial configuration. The current config may not work properly.
setting.blog.cookie.placeholder: Paste the platform cookie here. For your privacy and security, do not share it with anyone.
setting.blog.cookie.readonly.tip: This data is generated automatically by web authorization and stored only in your local browser. It is read-only. For your security, do not share it with anyone.
setting.blog.searchKeyword.placeholder: Enter a search keyword for [{title}], then press Enter or move focus away to continue
knowledgeSpaceEnabled.Tips: After validation succeeds, the available [[knowledge-space-title]] list will be loaded. You can then change the default [[knowledge-space-title]] and save it.
publisher.picbed.picgo.not.install: This feature requires the PicGo plugin 1.9.0+ . Install it from the marketplace first.
preference.setting.allowChangeSlug.tips: Warning: the slug is the unique identifier of the article. If you allow changes, you must keep it unique yourself or publishing may fail. If you are not sure, use the default auto-generated slug.
setting.wordpress.username.tip: WordPress login name. Note: this is the login name, not the display nickname.
setting.wordpress.apiUrl.tip: The WordPress XML-RPC endpoint, usually https://<your-wordpress-site>/xmlrpc.php
setting.cnblogs.home.tip: Your Cnblogs home page, usually https://www.cnblogs.com/<your-username>
setting.cnblogs.username.tip: Your Cnblogs MetaWeblog username, usually the same as your Cnblogs login name
setting.cnblogs.password.tip: Your Cnblogs MetaWeblog access token. You can find it in your Cnblogs backend settings.
v2.nav.title: Settings Navigation
v2.quickPublish.desc: Quick publish keeps the main content in focus. Settings are accessed from the low-emphasis entry in the top-right corner.
v2.quickPublish.empty.noDocument.title: Open a document first
v2.quickPublish.empty.noDocument.desc: The V2 main view reads the current document context to show available targets and later publish status.
v2.quickPublish.empty.noPlatforms.title: No enabled platforms yet
v2.quickPublish.empty.noPlatforms.desc: Open settings from the top-right corner, add and enable at least one platform, then use quick publish.
v2.quickPublish.tooltip.unauthorized: This platform is not authorized. Open settings in the top-right corner, then finish authorization in account settings before using quick publish.
v2.quickPublish.error.previewConfigMissi: Platform configuration is missing, cannot get the preview link
v2.quickPublish.error.publishConfigMissi: Platform configuration is missing, cannot publish
v2.quickPublish.error.deleteConfigMissin: Platform configuration is missing, cannot delete
v2.publish.desc.preparingDelete.named: Preparing to delete the published record for {name}.
v2.publish.desc.preparingDelete.default: Preparing to delete the published record.
v2.publish.desc.updating.named: Updating the content on {name}, please wait.
v2.publish.desc.deleting.named: Deleting the published record on {name}, please wait.
v2.publish.desc.previewReady.named: {name} already has a published record and can be opened directly.
v2.publish.desc.previewReady.default: A published record already exists and can be opened directly.
v2.publish.desc.updateFailed.named: Updating {name} failed. Check the error details.
v2.publish.desc.updateFailed.default: Update failed. Check the error details.
v2.publish.desc.deleteFailed.named: Deleting {name} failed. Check the error details.
v2.publish.desc.deleteFailed.default: Delete failed. Check the error details.
v2.publish.desc.publishFailed.named: Publishing to {name} failed. Check the error details.
v2.publish.desc.publishFailed.default: Publish failed. Check the error details.
v2.publish.desc.idle: Choose a platform to start publishing.
v2.card.confirm.deleteText: Delete this published content? You will need to publish again afterward.
v2.account.title: Account List
v2.account.desc: Review account status, enable or disable platforms, and open platform configuration.
v2.account.empty.title: No configured accounts yet
v2.account.empty.desc: Click "Add Account" in the top-right corner to create a platform configuration.
v2.account.action.deletePending: Delete cleanup will be connected in a later phase
v2.platformSelect.title: Select Platform
v2.platformSelect.desc: Choose the preset platform to connect. Account creation still finishes inside the current workspace shell.
v2.platformSelect.itemDesc: The preset key is used first. If it already exists, a new key is generated automatically.
v2.platformConfig.title: Platform Configuration
v2.platformConfig.desc: This screen bridges existing platform forms through real DOM and no longer depends on iframe settings pages.
v2.platformConfig.loading.title: Loading Platform Configuration
v2.platformConfig.loading.desc: Reading platform metadata and bridge components.
v2.platformConfig.error.title: Failed to Load Platform Configuration
v2.platformConfig.error.notFound: The related platform configuration was not found
v2.platformConfig.value.save: Directly reuses the existing configuration load and save chain
v2.platformConfig.value.validate: Directly reuses the existing validation logic and status write-back
v2.platformConfig.mounting.title: Mounting Bridge Form
v2.platformConfig.mounting.desc: Please wait while the platform form is loading.
v2.platformConfig.unsupported.title: This platform is not included in the M4 bridge scope yet
v2.platformConfig.unsupported.desc: Only WordPress and Cnblogs are bridged for now. More platform forms will be added later.
v2.picbed.title: PicBed Settings
v2.picbed.desc: PicBed services are saved per platform. This view no longer jumps to an external settings page. All changes are written directly into the current platform configuration.
v2.picbed.loading.title: Loading PicBed Configuration
v2.picbed.loading.desc: Reading platform configuration and PicBed capabilities.
v2.picbed.error.title: Failed to Load PicBed Configuration
v2.picbed.empty.title: No configurable platforms yet
v2.picbed.empty.desc: Add at least one platform account in Account Settings first.
v2.picbed.group.enabled.title: Enabled Platforms
v2.picbed.group.enabled.desc: These platforms are currently available for publishing. Changes here will affect later image upload behavior.
v2.picbed.group.disabled.title: Disabled Platforms
v2.picbed.group.disabled.desc: The PicBed strategy for these platforms will take effect after the account is enabled.
v2.picbed.environment.title: Current Environment
v2.picbed.environment.picgoAvailable: The PicGo plugin is available and can be used as an optional PicBed service.
v2.picbed.environment.picgoUnavailable: The PicGo plugin is not available, so related options remain disabled.
v2.picbed.support.none: This platform does not support extra PicBed services
v2.preference.title: Preference Settings
v2.preference.desc: These switches write directly to the current preference configuration. Except for "Use V2 UI", changes take effect immediately.
v2.preference.group.content.title: Content Processing
v2.preference.group.content.desc: Control the basic title and content processing behavior before publishing.
v2.preference.group.menu.title: Menu Entries
v2.preference.group.menu.desc: Control the visibility of menu entries in the SiYuan plugin environment.
v2.preference.group.experimental.title: Experimental Features
v2.preference.group.experimental.desc: Experimental features may affect overall UI behavior. Toggle them carefully.
v2.preference.item.fixTitle.desc: Remove automatic numbering and redundant suffixes from titles before publishing.
v2.preference.item.keepTitle.desc: Keep the original document title as much as possible without overriding it.
v2.preference.item.removeFirstH1.desc: Remove the first H1 from exported content to avoid duplicate titles.
v2.preference.item.removeMdWidgetTag.des: Remove widget-related markup from Markdown before publishing.
v2.preference.item.ignoreBlockRef.desc: Ignore block references during publishing to reduce cross-document dependency content in the final body.
v2.preference.item.allowChangeSlug.desc: Allow the article slug to be changed. A confirmation dialog is shown the first time it is enabled.
v2.preference.item.showDocQuickMenu.desc: Show the quick publish entry in the document context.
v2.preference.item.showQuickMenu.desc: Show the quick publish entry in the top-bar menu.
v2.preference.item.showSingleMenu.desc: Show the single publish entry in the top-bar menu.
v2.preference.item.showBatchMenu.desc: Show the batch publish entry in the top-bar menu.
v2.preference.item.showAIMenu.desc: Show the AI tools entry in the top-bar menu.
v2.preference.item.showExtendMenu.desc: Show the extensions entry in the top-bar menu.
v2.preference.item.showArticleManageMenu: Show the article management entry in the plugin menu.
v2.preference.item.useV2UI.label: Use V2 UI
v2.preference.item.useV2UI.desc: Enable the V2 interface as the preferred UI. A full restart is required for complete effect.
